key_specs: {"piece_count":"35 picture cards, 2 A4 backing boards","dimensions":"Cards approx. 6 x 5.5 cm; boards A4 length","materials":"Water-resistant plastic cards, foamex boards, VELCRO Brand fasteners","included_items":"35 home routine symbols, 2 display boards, hook-and-loop attached","other":"Shows up to 10 cards at once"}
product_description_under_30_words: Thirty-five durable home-routine picture cards with two A4 backing boards and Velcro fasteners. Displays up to ten steps of the day at once.
ember_verdict: If photos on the fridge have not stuck, this is the grown-up version — same idea, less DIY faff. At nearly three, independence wants a visible plan: shoes, teeth, coat, bag. You are not policing a perfect day; you are giving them something to point at when words bounce off. Buy only if real photos did not land.
personalization_hint: likely to be wanting to do the jobs themselves and getting stuck at the same transition every morning
why_it_fits: Only mainstream UK set that covers a full home day with rearrangeable cards — matches whole-routine brief without school-only symbols.
caveats: Generic symbols, not your child's room — photos may still beat this. Ten-card display limit on two boards.
gift_note: Parent-buy utility; not a gift unless parents asked for it.
ownership_note: Try phone photos of your real routine first — skip if that already works.
safety_notes: Cards are small — supervise around younger siblings; store unused symbols out of reach.
review_quality_note: Strong Amazon volume for Create Visual Aids SKU; brand site shows fewer aggregated reviews.
evidence_sources: Create Visual Aids product page | Amazon UK reviews
preloved_signal_note: Cards hold up; check Velcro and missing symbols in pre-loved sets.
substitute_if_unavailable: Create Visual Aids Now and Next Board plus 35 symbols
evidence_notes: Only mainstream UK set that covers a full home day with rearrangeable cards — matches whole-routine brief without school-only symbols.

key_specs: {"piece_count":"12 out-the-door visuals, 2 green backing boards","dimensions":"Visuals 5.5 x 4.5 cm","materials":"Plastic visuals, foamex boards, VELCRO Brand fasteners","included_items":"Lunchbox, water, bag, coat, shoes, trainers, PE kit and other leave-the-house symbols","other":"Designed for door-side checking"}
product_description_under_30_words: Door-side chart with twelve removable leave-the-house picture cards on two green boards. Child checks bag, coat and shoes without you repeating the list.
ember_verdict: The front-door moment is where nearly-three independence meets your lateness. This narrows the job to one strip: what goes out with us. Not a whole-day timetable — that is the point. Hang it where they actually leave, not on a bedroom wall they ignore.
personalization_hint: likely to be dawdling at the door or forgetting the same item every time you leave
why_it_fits: Focused leave-the-house strip beats a full timetable when the pain is coats, bags and shoes at the threshold.
caveats: Only one brand-site review at research date — evidence is specialist, not mass-market.
gift_note: Parent-buy only — too specific for unsolicited gifting.
ownership_note: A photo strip by the door may be enough; buy if repetition is wearing you down.
safety_notes: Small cards — keep away from babies; supervise first weeks until the habit lands.
review_quality_note: Single brand-site review; product spec verified on retailer page.
preloved_signal_note: Compact set; easy to sanitise and reuse.
substitute_if_unavailable: Create Visual Aids Out The Door Visuals symbol set plus existing board
evidence_notes: Focused leave-the-house strip beats a full timetable when the pain is coats, bags and shoes at the threshold.

product_name: Desktop Visual Timer - Black Silicone Case
key_specs: {"piece_count":"1 desktop timer","dimensions":"Compact desk timer with silicone case","materials":"Plastic timer, silicone protective case","included_items":"60-minute visual countdown timer","other":"Silent operation option"}
product_description_under_30_words: Sixty-minute desktop visual countdown timer with black silicone case. Red disk shrinks so a nearly-three can see how long teeth or getting dressed should take.
ember_verdict: Cards show what; a timer shows how long. Useful when brushing teeth or getting dressed turns into a stand-off — two minutes visible beats you counting down. Pair with photos or cards, not instead of them. Re-check price at checkout; ex-VAT listing confuses quick scans.
personalization_hint: likely to be stretching one step and needing a visible end point before moving on
why_it_fits: Adds time visibility to routine cards — the missing piece when a child understands the job but not how long it lasts.
caveats: £5.95 delivery under £45 on Sensory Direct. Ex-VAT price shown prominently on site.
gift_note: Utility buy for parents mid-routine battles.
ownership_note: Phone timer works first — buy if you want a always-on bathroom or kitchen clock.
safety_notes: Keep off high shelves child might climb to reach.
review_quality_note: Specialist SEN retailer; fewer consumer reviews than Amazon timer clones.
evidence_sources: Sensory Direct product page | Sensory Direct visual aids category
preloved_signal_note: Timers common on eBay; check battery and case condition.
evidence_notes: Adds time visibility to routine cards — the missing piece when a child understands the job but not how long it lasts.

product_name: Now and Next Board plus 35 symbols for home
key_specs: {"piece_count":"35 home symbols, 1 Now and Next board","dimensions":"Board foamex; symbols durable plastic","materials":"Water-resistant plastic symbols, VELCRO Brand fasteners","included_items":"Two-step board plus home routine symbol set","other":"Shows only next two activities"}
product_description_under_30_words: Now and Next board with thirty-five home picture symbols. Shows the current task and what comes straight after — built for transition meltdowns, not full-day planning.
ember_verdict: When the whole day feels like too much, shrink the horizon. Now: shoes. Next: park. That is often enough at nearly three. Use this for the sticky hand-offs — tablet off, coat on, leaving the house — not as your only routine system.
personalization_hint: likely to be stalling when one fun thing ends and the next boring step begins
why_it_fits: Two-step format matches transition pain better than a ten-step wall chart for many toddlers.
caveats: Overlaps symbol set with full timetable — do not buy both unless you need both formats.
ownership_note: Two printed photos and blu-tack can trial Now/Next before spending.
safety_notes: Small symbols — supervise around younger siblings.
review_quality_note: Standard Create Visual Aids SKU; spec verified on brand page.
preloved_signal_note: Board and symbols survive well second-hand.
substitute_if_unavailable: Create Visual Aids Now and Next Board plus Pick and Mix symbols
evidence_notes: Two-step format matches transition pain better than a ten-step wall chart for many toddlers.

key_specs: {"piece_count":"2 A4 planners, 35 task cards, velcro dots, storage bag","dimensions":"Planner A4; task cards 3.7 x 3.7 cm","materials":"Matt-laminated card, cotton storage bag","included_items":"Separate morning and evening planners with illustrated flap cards","other":"Adult setup required — cut flaps and attach velcro"}
product_description_under_30_words: Morning and evening A4 routine planners with thirty-five illustrated velcro task cards. Child closes each flap when done — progress they can see without reading.
ember_verdict: Prettier than clinic boards — useful if mornings and bedtimes are the only war zones. The flap-close mechanic gives a nearly-three a finished-job feeling your fridge photos might lack. Adult prep night one: cut, velcro, customise. Not for whole-day planning.
personalization_hint: likely to be needing a visible win when a step is done before moving to the next morning task
why_it_fits: Morning/evening split with tactile flap closure suits independence pride at this age better than abstract symbol strips alone.
caveats: Setup faff on arrival. Premium price versus Create Visual Aids bundles. Small review base.
gift_note: Parent-buy; pretty packaging but still a routine utility.
ownership_note: Skip if Create Visual Aids morning/bedtime boards return to stock at lower price.
safety_notes: Velcro dots are small — attach firmly; supervise setup.
review_quality_note: Small but strong review average; parents praise morning calm, note setup time.
evidence_sources: Little Goose product page | Little Sensory Home listing
preloved_signal_note: Laminated planners wear well; check velcro and missing cards.
substitute_if_unavailable: Create Visual Aids Morning and Bedtime Routine Chart Bundle
evidence_notes: Morning/evening split with tactile flap closure suits independence pride at this age better than abstract symbol strips alone.
